title: Future Stars working towards a better tomorrow
bodyText:
FOOTBALL NEWS - The Thembalethu-based side Future Stars FC resumed their George Local Football Agency (Geolfa) Promotional League fixtures on a high after the Easter break, registering two impressive victories at the Lawaaikamp Stadium.


> On Saturday 6 April, they were up against the struggling Tigers FC, beating them 2-0, and on Sunday afternoon, 7 April, the promising team defeated Paris United 2-0.


They dominated the game with fast-paced play, creating more chances into the box and scoring two beautiful goals.

Future Stars was formed and started by the soccer legends Zolani Makendlana and Thabiso Booysen early in 2021 with the main aim to give kids a healthy alternative for a life on the streets, which usually involve drug and alcohol abuse.

The Stars, that now have five divisions (u.13, u.15, u.17, u.19 and the senior team), are currently plying their trade in Promotional League where they are fighting their way up to gain promotion to the Domestic League.

Makendlana and Booysen told George Herald that they are pleased about the positive feedback from local stakeholders and are especially grateful for the support of the local businesses that provided the team with news tracksuits that contribute to a professional image.

The Future Stars Football Club team pose for an official photo in their new team tracksuits. Photo: Zama Gagu


> Anyone who wants to join or contribute towards the club can contact Zolani Makendlana on 078 218 6096 or Thabiso Booysen on 062 450 7527.


The Future Stars team managers, Zolani Makendlana and Thabiso Booysen, also donned their new tracksuits. Photo: Zama Gagu

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
